pattern: Counting words that begin with a given prefix is a direct per-word prefix check with no need for any shared trie structure at this scale.
hints: There is no trick here -- for each word, check whether it starts with the given prefix and tally how many do.
A word can only start with the prefix if the word is at least as long as the prefix itself.
Most languages give you a built-in starts-with check; use it directly instead of manually slicing and comparing substrings.

edge_inputs.description: two of four words share the prefix
prefix length equal to a word's full length still counts as a match
prefix longer than every word, so nothing can match
prefix equals some words in full while others only share the start

stress.description: random lowercase words and a random prefix, some engineered to match
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    pref_len = rng.randint(1, 10)
    pref = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(pref_len))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 100)
        if rng.random() < 0.5 and length >= pref_len:
            word = pref + ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(length - pref_len))
        else:
            word = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(length))
        words.append(word)
    return [words, pref]
